stories.title: Fear, defiance, and anger: Iranians describe life under bombardment
stories.description: In messages to NPR, Tehran residents describe largely deserted streets roamed by paramilitary officials and vigilantes. They say security forces are banning gatherings for Nowruz, the Persian new year, this week.

stories.title: Israel and Iran attack gas facilities. And, Cesar Chavez accused of sexual abuse, rape
stories.description: Attacks on gas facilities by Israel and Iran have escalated the war and impacted global markets. And, renowned union leader and labor rights advocate Cesar Chavez is accused of sexual abuse and rape.

stories.title: Iran attacks world's largest liquified natural gas complex in Qatar
stories.description: Iran struck Qatar's massive gas facility after Israel targeted Iran's gas field, putting global energy supplies at risk.

stories.title: What did President Trump know about Iran before going to war
stories.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Nate Swanson, former director for Iran at the National Security Council, about what President Trump understood about Iran before going to war.

stories.title: Japan's prime minister visits the White House under shadow of Iran war
stories.description: Japan Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi will be the first U.S. ally to visit the White House since President Trump asked for help in sending ships to patrol the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Why Trump's attacks and threats to Iran's Kharg Island are a big deal
stories.description: If the U.S. follows through on its threats to bomb the oil assets on Kharg Island, the war would likely escalate and oil prices would increase exponentially.

stories.title: Japan's PM to visit Trump as president continues pleas for help with Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: As Japan's Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi prepares for her first White House visit, President Donald Trump calls for Japan and other allies to send warships to the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: NPR's Leila Fadel talks about her experience in Iraq amid the Iran war
stories.description: NPR's Leila Fadel discusses what she was hoping to learn and what she found during her recent trip to Iraq amid the war with Iran that has engulfed the region.

stories.title: Israel and Iran attack gas facilities in a major escalation that rattles markets
stories.description: President Trump said that Israel acted alone when it struck the South Pars gas field in Iran. Trump threatened Iran to halt its attacks on Qatar's facilities or face a "blow up" at South Pars.

stories.title: Trump threatens to blow up 'entirety' of major Iran gas field if it attacks Qatar again
stories.description: The attacks led to a spike of more than 25% in gas prices in Europe on Thursday.

stories.title: Three Palestinian women killed in beauty salon during Iranian missile attack
stories.description: Others were injured when the salon in the southern West Bank was hit, while a Thai worker was killed by falling shrapnel in Israel.

stories.title: 'Very difficult to stop': BBC visits scene of Iran cluster bomb strike on Israel
stories.description: An elderly couple was killed after a bomb flew into their apartment in central Israel and exploded.

stories.title: Why the Strait of Hormuz matters so much in the Iran war
stories.description: Iran says it will "set fire" to ships trying to sail through the world's most vital oil transit point.

stories.title: Iran's intelligence minister Esmail Khatib killed in air strike
stories.description: Israel said it had killed Khatib, whose death was confirmed by Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian on Wednesday.

stories.title: Surge in US gas prices deepens political peril for Trump over Iran
stories.description: The odds of a quick resolution to the war appear to be dwindling by the day, and the political stakes for the president are only growing.

stories.title: Wary allies show there's no quick fix to Trump's Iran crisis
stories.description: European leaders are hesitant to help Trump secure the Strait of Hormuz, but they know inaction on the Iran war is not really an option.

stories.title: Israel says 'limited' ground operations under way in Lebanon
stories.description: Troops are targeting "Hezbollah strongholds" in south Lebanon, where strikes reportedly killed nine people on Monday.

stories.title: How might the UK support shipping in the Strait of Hormuz?
stories.description: Ben Chu from BBC Verify has been looking at what role the Royal Navy could play in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Who wants what from the Iran war?
stories.description: Most people, although not everybody, want this war to end as quickly as possible. But on what terms?

stories.title: What Iranians are being told about the war
stories.description: Iranian state media's coverage of the war includes inflations of enemy casualties and digital manipulation intended to glorify Iran.

stories.title: Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last?
stories.description: Conflict has spread across the Middle East since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran on 28 February.

stories.title: How Iranians are evading internet blocks to contact family abroad
stories.description: Iranians are finding tech-savvy ways to get through the regime's restrictions on phone and internet connections.

stories.title: Under drone fire, exiled Kurds wait to confront Iranian regime
stories.description: A group of fighters based in Iraq say they are "ready to go home", as they face drone attacks from Tehran.

stories.title: As hopes of regime change in Iran fade, Netanyahu faces political test
stories.description: Israeli leaders are framing the bombing campaign as having transformed the Middle East in their favour as pressure to end the conflict builds.

stories.title: Middle East war escalates after attack on key Iranian gas field
stories.description: #Israel hit Iranian's pat of the South Pars #gas field, the world's largest offshore reservoir #Tehran shares with #Doha. In retaliation for the strikes, #Iran ramped up its attacks on Gulf countries' energy infrastructures, sending #oil prices soaring.

stories.title: Steven Everts: For Europeans, war on Iran 'a diversion' and 'giant gift to Putin'
stories.description: Genie Godula is pleased to welcome Steven Everts, Director of European Union Institute for Security Studies (EUISS). According to Mr. Everts, the current conflict is an unnecessary war defined by cycles of retaliation that risk expanding beyond their original scope, with serious ...

stories.title: REPLAY: Pentagon press conference on war in Middle East
stories.description: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Thursday there is no "time frame" for ending the US-Israeli war against Iran, which was launched three weeks ago. "We wouldn't want to set a definitive time frame," Hegseth told a news conference, adding that "we're very much on track" and t...

stories.title: Energy prices soar, Iran and US trade threats after Qatar gas hit
stories.description: Oil and gas prices soared Thursday after Iran hit the world's largest liquefied natural gas (LNG) facility in Qatar and threatened to destroy the region's energy infrastructure. Donald Trump, whose country started the war alongside Israel with their attack on Iran on February 28,...

stories.title: Iran war exposes Gulf economies' food, water vulnerability
stories.description: The spiralling conflict in Iran has highlighted the potential vulnerability of Gulf states when it comes to food and water security. Countries like Bahrain and Qatar depend heavily on desalinated water, and strikes on desalination plants have sparked concern about water supplies....

stories.title: Millions of children out of school: Yemen's education system on its knees
stories.description: Since 2015 and the outbreak of war between the Houthis, who control the north of Yemen, and the internationally recognised government in the south, the country's education system has been steadily collapsing. According to UNICEF, nearly 4 million Yemeni children are currently out...

stories.title: Iran: Saudi Arabia warns of 'military action' over repeated attacks
stories.description: Saudi Arabia warned that it had 'reserved the right to take 'military actions' over #Iran's repeated attacks on the kingdom, as fresh trikes hit Riyadh during a summit of regional foreign ministers. The comment marks a turn in the #Gulf's reaction to Iran's strikes on its neighbo...

stories.title: Iran women's football team return after asylum tussle
stories.description: The Iranian women's football team, whose plight has become embroiled in the Middle East war, returned back to Iran on Wednesday where they were promised a welcome ceremony in Tehran. Seven members of the delegation had sought asylum in Australia last week after their decision not...

stories.title: Central banks confront spectre of inflation as Iran war escalates
stories.description: Strikes on the natural gas production facilities of Iran and Qatar have sent energy prices soaring, with central bankers around the world struggling to set monetary policy amid a highly uncertain inflationary outlook. We take a closer look. Also in this edition: the French shipbu...

stories.title: Trump threatens to strike world's largest gas field if Iran attacks Qatar again
stories.description: US President Donald Trump pledged the US would “massively blow up the entirety” of the world’s largest gas field if Iran attacks Qatar again. Trump made his threat on social media Wednesday night against Iran’s South Pars natural gas field after Iranian missiles hit Qatar. The Ir...

stories.title: Who is running Iran? Top leadership decimated by US-Israeli strikes
stories.description: Iran suffered a fresh blow Wednesday with the killing of its intelligence minister as the new supreme leader, yet to be seen since replacing his slain father, vowed to avenge the death of the country's security chief. Israel has killed one senior Iranian leader after another in a...

stories.title: Iraq caught in crossfire of Middle East war
stories.description: Iraq has been drawn into the Middle East war triggered by the US-Israel attack on its neighbour Iran on February 28. Strikes have targeted Iran-backed groups, which in turn have claimed near daily attacks on US interests in Iraq and across the region.

stories.title: EU summit to focus on wars in the Middle East and Ukraine
stories.description: European Union leaders are holding a summit in Brussels on Thursday for talks on the Iran war, energy prices, migration and an enormous loan for war-ravaged Ukraine being held up by Hungary. Many of those leaders have deflected entreaties by US President Donald Trump to send mili...

stories.title: Iran causes 'extensive' damage to Qatar gas hub, sparks Trump warning
stories.description: Qatar reported "extensive" damage on Thursday to the site of the world's largest liquefied natural gas (LNG) facility following Iranian strikes, sparking fears for global energy supplies and fresh threats from Donald Trump against Iran. Tehran carried out attacks on Qatar's huge ...

stories.title: Child among survivors pulled from rubble of US-Israeli attack in Iran
stories.description: Video from Iran’s Red Crescent showed a child among survivors pulled from the wreckage of a US-Israeli air attack.

stories.title: China’s silence on Iran reveals its true priorities
stories.description: Beijing's muted response shows that when core interests are at stake, even close partners are expendable.

stories.title: Why are Iran’s South Pars gasfield, Qatar’s Ras Laffan, so significant?
stories.description: Israel's strike on South Pars and Iran's attack on Ras Laffan escalate energy crisis, deepen regional tensions.

stories.title: Video: Why Iran’s attack on Qatar’s gas fields matters
stories.description: Gas prices have spiked after Israel’s attack on Iran’s main gas field prompted Iran to attack Qatari gas facilities.

stories.title: The US–Israel war with Iran will not end with a clear victor
stories.description: An arrangement that allows each side to claim it achieved its core goals is the most likely path out.

stories.title: Iran’s dilemma in war and peace
stories.description: Iran’s regional ambitions are increasingly constrained by military limits, economic pressure, and internal fragility.

stories.title: Could Iran war trigger the next global food shock?
stories.description: From factories to supermarket shelves, the Iran war is disrupting global supply chains.

stories.title: Conflict shifts to ‘energy war’ as Iran targets Gulf oil and gas
stories.description: Following Israel’s South Pars strike, Iran has targeted Gulf energy sites to pressure an end to the conflict.

stories.title: What did Arab and Muslim ministers discuss in Riyadh meeting on Iran?
stories.description: Foreign ministers formulate a unified response to Iranian strikes, asserting their right to self-defence.

stories.title: Are Iran’s athletes political pawns?
stories.description: Samantha Johnson looks at how Iran’s women footballers became caught in the middle of geopolitics.

stories.title: Iran hits Qatar gas operations in response to Israeli attack
stories.description: Qatar’s main gas facility is among the energy sites targeted by Iran after its own main gas field was hit in an attack.

stories.title: Strikes kill two PMF fighters in northern Iraq amid wider war, group says
stories.description: Strikes come hours after pro-Iran armed group Kataib Hezbollah announces conditional suspension of US embassy attacks.

stories.title: US East Asian allies in legal quandary as Trump seeks help in the Middle East
stories.description: US President Donald Trump is expected to discuss the Strait of Hormuz with Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi.

stories.title: Iran to ‘boycott’ World Cup in US but will not withdraw from 2026 event
stories.description: Iran scheduled to play their three FIFA World Cup 2026 group games in United States, but Mexico is an alternative.

stories.title: Trump denies knowledge of Israeli gas field attack
stories.description: Donald Trump said the US was unaware of Israel’s South Pars strike and warned Iran against targeting Qatar.

stories.title: Ministerial meeting calls on Iran to ‘immediately and unconditionally’ halt attacks in Gulf
stories.description: A ministerial meeting, hosted by Saudi Arabia, called on Iran to “immediately and unconditionally” halt attacks against Gulf countries in the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, the Saudi Arabian Foreign Ministry said on Thursday. The conflict began with US-Israeli strikes on Ir...

stories.title: ‘Baseless characterisation’: FO rejects remarks by Israel’s ambassador to India
stories.description: The Foreign Office (FO) on Thursday issued a statement rejecting the comments made by Israel’s ambassador to India, calling them a “baseless characterisation”. The FO statement referred to a video clip of the Israeli ambassador circulating on social media and said: “We firmly rej...

stories.title: Press review: Europe seeks to block US-Russia thaw as Russia eyes role in US-Iran talks
stories.description: Top stories from the Russian press on Friday, June 6th

stories.title: Israel to continue striking Beirut until Lebanon disarms Hezbollah — defense minister
stories.description: "Agreements must be honored and the Lebanese government does not do what is required, Israel will continue to act, and with great force," Israel Katz said

stories.title: Orban sees EU bracing for another decade of countering Russia amid proxy war in Ukraine
stories.description: Commenting on the Ukraine crisis, the Hungarian PM said that Brussels viewed Ukraine as "the first line of European defense" where pan-European interests were currently being defended

stories.title: Israel strikes Hezbollah targets in Beirut suburbs — IDF
stories.description: Prior to that, the Israeli army issued a warning for this neighborhood, telling residents to leave their homes immediately

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
